'''Viliami Sikalamu''' "'''Sika'''" '''Manu''' (born 22 January 1987) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at international level, being a member of the World Cup winning [[New Zealand national rugby league team|Kiwi]] team who beat Australia in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup Final]].

At club level played for the [[Melbourne Storm]], with whom he won the [[2012 NRL Grand Final]], and also the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
He was born in [[Wellington, New Zealand]].

Manu is of full Tongan descent and as a result is eligible both for New Zealand and Tonga. His older brother Filipe was also contracted to the Storm.<ref name=prove /> He is cousin to the Super 15 Rugby union player from the Highlanders, [[Nasi Manu]]. He is also cousin to up and coming Rugby superstar David Manu, located in the Eastern Suburbs.Manu was educated at [[Upper Hutt College]], where he played for the school's First XV rugby team.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/dominion-post/sport/college-sport/682851/Victory-sealed-in-extra-time |title=Victory sealed in extra time |publisher=Stuff |date=17 March 2009 |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
He played for the [[Randwick Kingfishers]], Petone Panthers and the [[Upper Hutt Tigers]] in the [[Wellington Rugby League]].<ref name=prove>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10683967&ref=rss|title=League: Time for Manu to prove himself|author=Deane, Steve|date=30 October 2010|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=11 November 2011}}</ref> Manu was a part of the [[Junior Kiwi|2004 Junior Kiwi]] side who twice defeated the England Under 18s Academy. Following this series, Manu signed a NRL contract with the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>[http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-4979-0-0-0&sID=253118 Team of the Century - Week 6] ''Wellington Rugby League''</ref>
[[File:Sikamanu.JPG|thumb|left|Sika Manu]]

===Melbourne Storm===
Manu made his first [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in 2007. He has since played over 50 games for the club. He played in the [[2008 NRL Grand Final]] defeat by Manly.<ref name="Grand final: As it happened">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24449388-23214,00.html|title=Grand final: As it happened|work=Fox Sports|date=2008-10-05|access-date=2008-10-05}}</ref>

Manu suffered a leg injury in the Storm's 2009 Round 21 clash with the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], thus ending his season. He ultimately missed the Grand Final win. Manu was selected to play for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] from the interchange bench in their loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]]. On 30 September 2012 he became part of the Storm's second premiership title, when they beat the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] 14-4 in the [[2012 NRL Grand Final]].

===Penrith Panthers===
On 5 June 2012 [[Penrith Panthers]] General Manager [[Phil Gould (rugby league)|Phil Gould]] announced that Manu had signed a three-year deal with the Penrith club, commencing from the start of the 2013 [[National Rugby League]] season.

===Hull F.C.===
On 13 August 2015, he signed a 3-year contract with [[Super League]] team [[Hull F.C.]] starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/manu-signs-super-league-deal-18590/ |title=Manu signs Super League deal |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=13 August 2015 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171022033458/https://www.zerotackle.com/manu-signs-super-league-deal-18590/ |archive-date=22 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://hullfc.com/article/41228/world-cup-winner-manu-to-join-hull-fc-from-2016|title = The Official Hull FC Website | Hull FC}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

===International career===
[[File:Sika Manu New Zealand.jpg|thumb|right|Manu playing for the Kiwis]]

====New Zealand====
Manu made his [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] debut in 2008. He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tongan]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squads]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Tug-of-war over Mason">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3935853,00.html|title=Tug-of-war over Mason|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-05|access-date=2008-08-06}}</ref><ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref> In October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#New Zealand|Kiwi]] squad and was part of the team that went on to win the tournament.<ref name="Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7656238.stm|title=Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref>

====Tonga====
In 2013 Sika, through his Tongan heritage, he made his début for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in their Pacific Rugby League International clash with fierce Pacific rivals [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].

Later in the year Sika represented Tonga in their unsuccessful [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign. He played in all 3 of Tonga's matches, scoring two tries. He scored both of his tries in [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]'s nail-biting 2 point defeat by [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] (unfortunately the only defeat of their World Cup campaign which ultimately ended Tonga's hopes of a knock-out round spot).

On 2 May 2015, Manu captained Tonga in their 2015 Polynesian Cup test-match against Pacific rivals Samoa. On 17 October 2015 Manu captained Tonga again this time in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|Asia-Pacific Qualifier]] match against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].

On 7 May 2016, Manu traveled down from Hull to Sydney to captain [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the 2016 Polynesian Cup<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-Frank-Pritchard-Sika-Manu-return/story-29203653-detail/story.html#ixzz47LbYe6hK|title=Hull FC: Frank Pritchard and Sika Manu to return to Australia and miss Challenge Cup game|work=Hull Daily Mail|access-date=1 May 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160501102446/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-FC-Frank-Pritchard-Sika-Manu-return/story-29203653-detail/story.html#ixzz47LbYe6hK|archive-date=1 May 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], where he started in the second row in the 18-6 loss at [[Parramatta Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/samoa-holds-on-in-pulsating-pacific-test/tabid/10874/newsid/95681/default.aspx|title=Samoa holds on in pulsating Pacific Test|date=7 May 2016|publisher=nrl.com|access-date=7 May 2016}}</ref>

In 2018 he announced his retirement from international rugby league.
